citation: Davies, M.E.P. and Böck, S. (2019). Temporal convolutional networks for musical audio beat tracking. Proc. 27th European Signal Processing Conference (EUSIPCO).
notes: Architecture per Davies & Böck 2019 Table I: 81-band log-magnitude spectrogram (12 bands/octave, 30-17kHz, 100fps), conv frontend (3x3+pool, 3x3+pool, 1x8 no pool), TCN dilations 2^0..2^10, kernel 5, 16 filters, ELU, spatial dropout 0.1.
Training: Adam lr=1e-3, batch=1, binary cross-entropy, ReduceLROnPlateau factor=0.2 (=1/5 per paper), early stop patience=50 (Davies & Böck 2019 Section II-B3).
Beat-only model (no downbeat head) per original paper. DBN decoding: BPM 55-215, transition lambda=100 (Krebs et al. 2015 defaults).
Requires datasets: ballroom, beatles, hainsworth, simac, smc under MIR_DATA_ROOT.
